programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part one of two. Walker and his team deal with a gang whose extortion ends in the murder of a wealthy Dallas family and its staff, and a daughter of a rich businessman is kidnapped

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio believes the death of a councillor's aide is not as clear-cut as it first appears - and his suspicions are confirmed by the subsequent discovery of a reporter's body in the boot of a stolen car. David Caruso, Emily Procter and Adam Rodriguez star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are forced to undergo psychological evaluations conducted by Dr Rachel Cranston (guest star Wendy Makkena) while investigating the death of a submarine's commanding officer. Suspicion falls on a journalist seen arguing with the man before his demise, and Tony discovers a link between the psychotherapist shadowing the agents and Ziva's predecessor Kate Todd. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble discovers that negligence caused the death of a salvage diver and jeopardises his own life when he befriends the girl accused of being a jinx

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: James Kincannon finds himself in need of Perry's help after not only being accused of jewel fencing, but also pushing a man out of a penthouse window

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team set up a stake-out at a storage facility to catch a thief suspected of selling hi-tech Navy equipment on the black market, but end up witnessing a murder. To make matters worse, $90million worth of radar gear goes missing while they are distracted. Meanwhile, Ducky asks Abby to run a series of mysterious blood tests, raising suspicions that he is harbouring a secret

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part two of two. The team tries to provide the evidence needed to convict a serial killer but faces a series of challenges and struggles to reach the courtroom in time

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is called to the scene when there is an attempt on the life of FBI agent TC Fornell, with the assailant identified as Navy seaman Tyler Brown. The pair team up and visit the bar where Brown worked and bump into their mutual ex-wife Diane Sterling, who was operating undercover as an employee suspected of stealing customers' identities. Joe Spano and Melinda McGraw guest star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. The agents learn the cash has been used to set up a bank robbery intended as a ruse to allow a bank employee to transfer $100million of the terrorists' money that has been frozen by the government in an offshore bank. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A suspect in a drug-smuggling case is seemingly mauled to death by his own dog, which is immediately scheduled to be destroyed - but Abby refuses to hand it over to Navy officials and launches her own investigation. Meanwhile, Gibbs continues to worry about Shepard's health, convinced she and Ducky are hiding something. Drama, starring Pauley Perrette, Mark Harmon and Lauren Holly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a Navy commander's wife, determining whether it is linked to her role as a therapist, or an act of terrorism since her husband's name has appeared on a Jihadist's target list. Elsewhere, the group discusses Halloween costumes, as well as Tony's recent dating habits. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team uncovers a fraudulent charity that preys on those searching for missing military personnel while investigating the murder of a retired Master Sergeant. Meanwhile, Bishop agonises over the results of her probation evaluation, and Tony contemplates adopting a stray cat that followed him home. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the cause of a hit-and-run incident in which a Petty Officer has been killed. Meanwhile, Gibbs works to clear a Navy Hospital Corpsman who has been indicted for illegally providing medical aid to victims of the crash, despite the injured insisting her techniques helped save their lives. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An enigmatic killer shoots dead a businessman spending Thanksgiving at a hotel in the city, then takes his glasses, jewellery and ID to Washington Dulles airport, which has been brought to a stand-still by a massive snowstorm. As the murderer's actions take effect, a terror alert is issued, leaving all northern US airports in a state of panic, and leaving it up to DiNozzo, Bishop and her husband - all of whom are trapped at Dulles by the weather - to bring the armed villain to justice. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherley

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee deals with his father's recent death by writing him a letter outlining the details of his latest investigation. The case involved tracking down a hactivist collective known as Krampus, who were thought to have been responsible for an incident in which the internet crashed all over Washington, DC. McGee and Abby brought in three known cyber-terrorists for questioning, and discover evidence that links the trio to an attempted breach of the Congress e-mail server and archive. However, when this line of inquiry reaches a dead-end, the team picks up a new lead that suggests an isolated hacker from Wisconsin may be responsible. This 'lone wolf' scenario does not seem plausible to McGee, though, as he recalls a lesson his late father taught him. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is left stranded on a deserted island when the plane crashes on the way to Toby and Happy's honeymoon

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Three weeks after becoming stranded on a deserted island, tensions run high among the agents, and in-fighting threatens their chances of being rescued

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The driver of a Ferrari is gunned down by a motorcyclist and the victim is identified as petty officer Colin Boxer, who was holding down a second job as a valet parker and had borrowed the sports car. The owner of the vehicle turns out to be Vijay Chaya, the billionaire head of a large corporation, and the team suspects a case of mistaken identity. During the course of the investigation, Gibbs chances on a medal awarded to Leroy Jethro Moore, the man he was named after and an estranged friend of his father. Guest starring Billy Dee Williams (The Empire Strikes Back) and Ralph Waite (The Waltons)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. First lieutenant Michael Torres gets involved in a fight before falling to the ground, and is found beaten to death the next morning. The only witness is his friend and colleague Captain Joe Westcott, who suffers from post-traumatic stress disorder and has trouble distinguishing reality from his past military experiences in the Middle East

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tony reviews satellite footage of rebel forces in the Middle East and spots Kersey among a group of insurgents, concluding that Joe Westcott's fear of him was well-founded. Back at HQ, Abby creates a 3D computer model of the attack that occurred in Afghanistan and Joe dons a virtual-reality headset to relive the traumatic events. Will it jog his memory?

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. Lydia's Navy captain father David Wade was shot dead during the attack and her mother is overseas, so Ziva offers to look after the girl, while Abby tries to jog her memory by creating facial reconstructions of the attackers based on some grainy CCTV footage. DiNozzo and McGee find links on Rosie's laptop to possible suspect John 'Diesel' Stevens

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the murder of a marine during a cruise. They discover he came from a famous and wealthy family but had turned his back on a life of privilege to join the military, and after the death of his father had inherited a large part of his business. His decision to change his will may have pushed one of his relatives to kill him to keep the firm in their control. Crime drama, guest-starring Diane Neal (Law & Order: Special Victims Unit)

Crime drama, guest starring comedian Bob Newhart

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The wife of a comedian is killed, but evidence suggests her partner may have been the intended target. Detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke, Barry Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella question the employees of a dock worker whose crushed remains have been found on Staten Island, before being led to another corpse - but suspects for both murders are thin on the ground. Meanwhile, an apparent hit-and-run turns into a complex puzzle for Danny and Aiden. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes, Carmine Giovinazzo and Vanessa Ferlito

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella have reason to suspect a police officer is involved in the murder of a wine store owner after evidence links him to a Hispanic gang present at the crime scene. Their investigations prove upsetting for Flack, as the man under suspicion is his old mentor

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Dan Stanwyck, an investigator working on a report into police corruption, is fatally shot the night before his findings are published. Taylor and Stella are convinced he was killed by a crooked officer whose wrongdoings were about to be exposed. Meanwhile, Danny and Aiden look into the case of a taxi driver stabbed to death in his cab

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate a hotel frequented by foreign diplomats after a woman's body is found wrapped in bedsheets. They tangle with a trade representative whose murky past suggests he could be the killer. Meanwhile, Danny argues with his boss over the corpse of a homeless man found covered with silver paint. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Taylor investigates the murder of a student who has been beaten and shot, and the discovery of heroin traces in his apartment leads him to suspect the killing was drug-related. The case becomes more urgent when it emerges that the victim's female room-mate has disappeared. Stella embarks on a harsh line of interrogation that results in her methods being called into question

Meanwhile, wise to Steve's monetary problems, Danny appoints himself as his personal financial adviser. Detective drama, starring Larry Lamb

programme.desc: Richard Sharpe becomes a pawn in a French plan to retain control of Spain, and finds himself facing execution for murder. Determined to regain his honour, he undertakes a dangerous quest to find the only person capable of clearing his name. Adaptation of Bernard Cornwell's novel, starring Sean Bean

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: The death of a policeman in a Soho strip club introduces Gene and the team to their new boss, detective 'Super Mac'. After months of silence, Alex again starts receiving flashes from the present - as well as the attentions of a mysterious admirer - while Chris tries to impress Shaz with his credentials as a new man. Philip Glenister and Keeley Hawes star

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: A car chase ends with a fatal crash, and Gene is accused of causing death by dangerous driving, leading to increased tension with the local community. As Alex takes up the fight to prove her boss's innocence, she becomes concerned that he is being led into corruption by Super Mac - and to add to her problems, she begins to doubt her mysterious stalker's offer of a way home

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: In the summer of 1966, the Northumbrian police come under increasing media scrutiny as Sunderland prepares to host a World Cup match involving the USSR, while campaigners protest against the proposed landing of the Polaris nuclear submarine at Jarrow docks. When an academic is found dead after a CND rally, Gently and Bacchus are dispatched to Durham University to investigate his background - and find themselves in the middle of a wave of social and sexual rebellion. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with Sarah Lancashire and Warren Clarke

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: An avid cyclist suffers a massive coronary at the top of a local mountain, but with astronomical levels of caffeine in his system, there is more to his death than meets the eye

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Phryne arrives at an idyllic vineyard to investigate a suspicious death, but the hostile townsfolk do everything they can to drive her out of town. Meanwhile, Hugh prepares for a perfect proposal. Australian crime drama set in the 1920s, starring Essie Davis and Hugo Johnstone

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A string of mysterious deaths occurs shortly after the arrival of Buffalo Bill Cody's Western Extravaganza in Toronto, and it falls to Murdoch to investigate the apparent murders. Meanwhile, Annie Oakley attracts the interest of his senior colleague. Yannick Bisson stars

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: An investigator arrives from Scotland Yard on the trail of infamous serial killer Jack the Ripper. Meanwhile, Murdoch takes dancing lessons so that he can escort Julia to a ball. Crime drama, starring Yannick Bisson as a Victorian sleuth

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: A wealthy land-owner's wife is strangled to death and her body dumped in a reservoir. Taggart and his partner Livingstone become suspicious when they discover the dead woman's husband was last seen preparing to leave for Switzerland - and carrying a large sum of money. Guest starring Alan Cumming

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The sleuthing 12th-century monk investigates a murder as the Abbey becomes involved in the war between King Stephen and the Empress Maud. programme.desc: CCTV footage proves Jay's girlfriend Cheryl was guilty of torching the home of troublesome lottery winners the Henderson family, but the team wonder whether the Sun Hill arsonist is still on the loose. Kezia sees a softer side to Smithy as he calmly solves a heated dispute and Lewis dreads living on his own when his gran announces she's going back to Jamaica. Sam tells ex-lovers Stuart and Phil she is to be their new boss

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Roger rescues probation officer Paul Brookes, who has been beaten and locked in the boot of his own car. Further investigations reveal the assailant's wife is a prisoner at Burnwood who claims she has been raped by Brookes. Gina turns up at court to give evidence, only to find the magistrate is an old flame, who promptly wipes the floor with her - before asking her out to dinner. Lewis finds a new home via the nurses' noticeboard at St Hugh's Hospital

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Heaton puts his team in the picture about the possible closure of Sun Hill when rumours reach the local press. Mickey and Jo investigate the death of a young mother with a history of drug use and discover she has been clean for some time but has fallen prey to a ruthless loan shark. Roger and Dan talk to a pub landlord who has been attacked at a fancy dress stag night

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: There's no turning back for Zain as he agrees to feed false information back to Sun Hill about the Colombian drug operation. Still wild about Kristen after their holiday together, he becomes indiscreet about his double life and rubs gangster Paul Haskew up the wrong way. Rob and Neil investigate a well-heeled man who appears to have assaulted his mother and Honey persuades Will to go away with her

programme.title: Oliver Twist
programme.desc: Part one of two. Adaptation of Charles Dickens' story. An orphan boy endures a horrible childhood in the workhouse, but yearns for more. After being sold to a cruel undertaker, he runs away to London - where he joins a group of pickpockets. Timothy Spall heads an all-star cast as Fagin, with support from Tom Hardy, Gregor Fisher, Sarah Lancashire, Sophie Okonedo, Julian Rhind-Tutt and William Miller in the title role

programme.title: Oliver Twist
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sikes escapes back to London with a badly wounded Oliver, while Mr Bumble declares his love for Mrs Corney - and captivated by the promise of social advancement, she agrees to marry him. Later, Nancy stumbles on a secret meeting, during which she overhears Monks telling Fagin he wants Oliver dead in two days

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Man Who Cried
programme.desc: Feature-length period drama about Abel Mason, an ordinary working man whose extramarital affair leads to murder, and prompts him to hit the road with his son in search of a new life. The pair head north and Abel eventually finds employment with a wealthy garage owner. Ciaran Hinds and Kate Buffery star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Winifred's eyes are opened to Poplar's seedy underworld when she cares for expectant prostitute Bridget Cole. Horrified by the young woman's lack of awareness, she draws on her teaching experience, and with help from Sgt Noakes sets out to promote safe sex to all local working girls. Barbara struggles to understand why expectant father Frank Robbins would pressure his wife to bear him a son, while Sister Monica Joan is feeling sidelined - until she finds herself unexpectedly assisting Shelagh with an emergency birth. Trixie invites herself along to a meeting between Tom and the Bishop - but things get tense when the idea of the couple moving to Newcastle is raised

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: Cosette has grown into a teenager, and to Valjean's dismay, questions her closeted life and is desperate for a new start in Paris. The opportunity presents itself when she meets Pontmercy's son Marius, who has turned his back on his family after learning the truth about his father, and now lives in a squalid tenement, where he catches the eye of the daughter of a criminal family. Javert ascends through the ranks of the police, and believes he is closing in on his goal

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Henry does little to hide his disappointment when his queen gives birth to a girl, with Anne aware that her power in court rests on producing a male heir. With the Tudor line remaining vulnerable, Cromwell decrees that everyone in public life must take an oath to recognise Henry's supremacy as head of the English Church, as well as the legality of his marriage to Anne Boleyn, but Thomas More refuses to swear allegiance and is committed to the Tower. Mark Rylance, Claire Foy, Damian Lewis and Anton Lesser star

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Part one of two. Walker is assigned to investigate the discovery of a young woman's skeleton, found underneath the patio of what used to be a boarding house, but is paired with Pat's ex-boyfriend Jeff. Drama from Widows creator Lynda LaPlante, starring David Hayman and Paul Kynman

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: A juggler seeks sanctuary at the abbey, desperate to escape an angry mob who believe he attacked and robbed a goldsmith. Sensing that events are not what they seem, Cadfael investigates - and it is not long before another body is found floating in the river. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

programme.desc: The emergency services attend a baby who has cut his throat on a glass. Plus, a woman is knocked to the ground and trampled after a dog spooks horses. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In this Children in Need special, Natasha Raskin-Sharp looks back at some of the famous faces who have taken part in the annual charity initiative and finds out how Pudsey Bear was created. She also offers hints and tips about buying and selling antiques and collectables.

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers get to grips with a specially designed pram for messy eating, a super-absorbent sponge that can soak up 12 times as much water as normal, and an anti-theft sports bag

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson returns to the Isle of Wight, the location of a previous property hunt, wanting to discover whether a couple he helped succeeded in finding a balance of home and business potential in their new setting. Back on the mainland, Jules travels to south-west Wales to meet a husband and wife who craved a sizable Welsh farmhouse, but who have found that a home is about so much more than bricks and mortar

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Presenter Jay Blades enlists the help of designer Sarah Peterson to give two side tables a stylish new look, while lighting expert Duncan McKean attempts to give two old record players a radical overhaul. For his own project, Jay attempts to make some money for nothing from two farmhouse stools. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The open road brings Charles Hanson and James Braxton - and their classic cars - to Hampshire. Along the way, Charles struggles with the art of signwriting and James meets a little owl. James is also drawn to an exotic alcohol container, while Charles' eye lands on an unusual pocket watch stand. The duo then head to the final auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which five pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: A jam-packed evening of socially distanced entertainment, with exclusive sketches, exciting performances, music, dance and a few surprises. Mel Giedroyc is joined by Alex Scott, Chris Ramsey and Stephen Mangan, who will all be co-hosting the fund-raising telethon for the first time. There are Strictly Come Dancing, EastEnders and Doctor Who-related surprises in store, while Matt Baker, Ade Adepitan and the team of six young riders make their way to the finishing line after 332 miles around the Goodwood Estate as part of The One Show's 2020 Rickshaw Challenge. Beverley Knight and Shawn Mendes perform, while The Repair Shop team gives one very special girl a surprise to treasure for ever

programme.title: A Simple Favour (2018)
programme.desc: Stephanie Smothers, a widowed single mother who runs a crafts and recipes vlog, becomes friends with Emily Nelson, a public relations director for a fashion company. Emily has a work crisis and asks Stephanie to babysit her son, but later goes missing amid suggestions from colleagues she has jetted off to Miami. Feeling perplexed, Stephanie attempts to get to the bottom of her friend's mysterious actions. Paul Feig's comedy thriller based on Darcey Bell's novel, starring Anna Kendrick, Blake Lively and Henry Golding

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ching-He Huang and Vivek Singh, and special guest Melanie C. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares the rhythms and rituals of her kitchen and reveals the inspiration behind many of her recipes. She begins with her take on bhorta, a dish of fried and mashed vegetables mainly associated with Bangladesh, to which she introduces a very British - but very familiar - ingredient. After creating a warm, spicy and aromatic dish of lamb shanks with wide noodles, Nigella uses very ripe bananas to make a rich and gooey chocolate tahini pudding, and then goes on to create a warming curry with a surprising ingredient

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker look ahead to the weekend's UEFA Nations League fixtures, including Belgium v England, Wales v Republic of Ireland, Slovakia v Scotland and Austria v Northern Ireland

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in Sutton Coldfield, Kent and Ipswich, then talk to the buyers about their plans

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams acquire antiques in Brighton with the help of experts Danny Sebastian and John Cameron, then take their items to auction in Bllingshurst. Presenter Charlie Ross also takes in the Royal Pavilion and discovers its connection to famous milliner Stephen Jones

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple find a property in the North Yorkshire countryside on their £400,000 budget, and he also traverses Stean Gorge on a series of high-wire cables

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues a sideboard, a chaise longue and an overcoat bound for the tip, before she and two others restyle them in the hope of making a profit for the owners

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: A Letchworth couple receive a surprise £4,500 garden makeover as a thank you from their son - a challenge that Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers find difficult to resist

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Lee Thompson, Pauline Black, Steve Brown, Barbara Dickson, Kimberly Wyatt, Cathy Dennis, Heather Small and Steve Hogarth

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a special celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, as a host of famous faces compete to win £10,000 for a charity of their choice. The contestants are Strictly Come Dancing professional dancers AJ Pritchard and his brother Curtis, journalist and newsreader Kate Silverton, TV presenter Anneka Rice, resident This Morning doctor Dr Ranj Singh, and Radio 1 DJ Dev Griffin

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Last week, it seemed the force was not with Jason and Luba as they performed their Star Wars paso doble, and they became the second couple to be voted off the show. Sadly, Nicola and Katya have also dropped out of the competition after Katya tested positive for Covid-19. That leaves nine couples to take the dance floor, now more aware than ever that as they learn their routines and face the judges, they have one more potential hurdle that could appear at any time. Presented by Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fifth episode, married couple Dan and Alison from Swansea face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund Blackadder, conniving courtier to Elizabeth I, takes in a spirited lad as his manservant - unaware the fellow is actually a woman out to make her fortune. Classic comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson, Miranda Richardson and Stephen Fry, with a guest appearance by Rik Mayall as Edmund's best friend Lord Flashheart

programme.title: Ant-Man (2015)
programme.desc: A thief attempting to go straight is recruited by a scientist who provides him with a suit that gives him the power to shrink. He must use his new skills to carry out a special mission - helping the inventor to steal back his miniaturisation technology from a ruthless businessman who intends to turn it into a deadly weapon. Superhero adventure, starring Paul Rudd, Michael Douglas and Evangeline Lilly

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a round-up of the latest fixtures, including Tennessee Titans v Indianapolis Colts

programme.title: Operation Avalanche (2016)
programme.desc: Two young CIA agents are sent to NASA, under the false pretence of making a documentary, to investigate reports of a mole. Once there, though, they become entangled in a much bigger conspiracy involving the Apollo 11 mission and the filming techniques of director Stanley Kubrick. Thriller, starring Matt Johnson, Owen Williams and Andrew Appelle

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow projects involving a listed Victorian lodge in Plaistow, London, an end terrace in Liverpool and a former pub in Blackburn

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks offer advice to two teams at the Highland Centre in Edinburgh, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to a camera obscura in the city

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is back on home turf in north Wales as he takes a journey exploring how his faith has shaped his life and his singing, catching up with composer Howard Goodall and the Rev Canon Mark Oakley along the way. Aled sings a couple of songs that are special to him and picks his favourite hymns for the programme, including Guide Me, O Thou Great Jehovah and How Great Thou Art

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore enlists the talents of master carpenter Norman Wilkinson, designer Daniel Heath and lighting guru Guy Trench to transform four items she has salvaged from the nation's tips into top-end treasured pieces

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple search for a country home close to the coast in Hampshire. While in the area, she also pays a visit the Mary Rose Museum

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Highlights from this year's charity fundraiser in which a line-up of familiar faces came together to help raise money for disadvantaged children around the UK

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live on coral reefs, including groupers, small predators that scientists believe are as intelligent as chimpanzees. These creatures have a remarkable aptitude for communication to the point where they can interact with other species including octopi and work together to catch prey. Plus, footage of a vast spawning event involving several different species of fish, coral and invertebrates, and time-lapse photography of coral polyps growing and dying, creating a structure visible from space

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison explores the powers of the great outdoors to provide a tonic in troubled times as she immerses herself in nature, from forest bathing to wild swimming. There are also films from the Countryfile archives about the health-giving prowess of the UK's green havens, including John Craven finding out how nature helped heal wartime wounds, and Anita Rani visiting a special farm giving respite to refugees

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before the judges decide who will be the third pair to leave the contest. With music by the Vamps

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra crosses into Will's world and finds it to be very different to her own, and after encountering the enigmatic Charles Latrom, the pair head to find a scholar to help get answers about Dust. Will is shocked to discover he has grandparents, but quickly realises he can't trust them, while a calculating Mrs Coulter pushes MacPhail to act after Graves' strong performance during the interrogation of a witch consul

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: The first episode of Steve McQueen's drama anthology based on the real-life experiences of London's West Indian community from the late-1960s to the mid-1980s. The film tells the story of the Old Bailey trial of Frank Crichlow, the owner of West Indian restaurant Mangrove in London's Notting Hill, who in 1970 was one of nine men and women wrongly arrested and charged with incitement to riot and affray after a protest about racial discrimination by the police. Starring Shaun Parkes, Letitia Wright and Malachi Kirby

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester United v Manchester City and Aston Villa v Birmingham City

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin revisits two sets of buyers who have successfully established new lives in the Devon countryside after appearing on Escape to the Country. First he is invited to the north of the county by an entrepreneurial couple whose rural aspirations needed to accommodate not only them but a pack of alpacas, paying guests and a grand piano. He then travels south to reunite with a couple at their picture-perfect home where they have found the space and time to indulge in their passions

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues another selection of items from the recycling centre, giving a little green chair to upholsterer Simion Hawtin-Smith and a dated display cabinet to upholsterer Katie Keat, while she transforms a pair of school chairs

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a set of bagpipes that once belonged to a man's grandfather, who served as a piper and a soldier in the Royal Scots during the First World War. Elsewhere, silversmith Brenton West helps to restore a Welsh crown of cultural significance that bears little resemblance to its former splendid self, clockwork genius Steve Fletcher takes on the repair of a quirky tortoise-shaped bell, and furniture restorer Will Kirk is thrilled to be presented with an enormous challenge - an antique wooden chair dating from the Carolean period

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter G. Lorraine Pascale cooks pan-fried mascarpone gnocchi with dreamy basil pesto, and Rachel Khoo serves grapefruit meringue tartlets

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes Jake Wood back to the Cornish coast, where the EastEnders star reminisces about a school adventure holiday he had there in 1980

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: With the wedding back on, it's time for Woody's 'groomspiel', and Big Mac has wild plans for it. However, Woody has made Tracy a promise he'll behave - which is going to be hard to keep. Meanwhile, new arrival Poppy and Arlo are getting on so well that Lindsay is jealous, even if Arlo is too innocent to realise

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: The five remaining potters are faced with their most technically demanding challenge when they have to make a decorative chandelier in bone china. For their Spot Test, steady hands are required when judges Kate Malone and Keith Brymer Jones assess their 'banding' skills, and for the Throw Down they have just 15 minutes on the wheel to make the largest closed sphere they can

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: The four remaining potters compete in the final with Kate Malone and Keith Brymer Jones presenting them with their most technically demanding challenge in the Main Make when they have to make an original 12-piece tea set out of porcelain. For their Spot Test, they must cut intricate and decorative designs into leather hard porcelain using a technique known as 'the devil's work', and for the Throw Down they have just 20 minutes to make three high-shouldered jugs - one of the hardest shapes to throw at the wheel

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents previously unseen finds from the show's travels round the country, visiting Muncaster Castle, the Bowes Museum and Norwich Cathedral. Antiques experts include Kate Bateman, Paul Laidlaw, Jonathan Pratt, James Lewis and Thomas Plant, with items ranging from a pair of Lalique vases to a 9.5mm projector. Paul also takes a tour around Powis Castle in Wales, finding out about their collection of Indian artefacts housed on site in the Clive Museum

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Backshall, Catherine Bohart, Ranj Singh and Meera Syal test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the week's ultimate winner finally declared

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Gethin Jones present analysis of the couples' progress, inviting a trio of celebrity superfans discuss all things ballroom

programme.title: A Special School
programme.desc: Observational documentary following the reality of life in Britain's biggest special school. In the first episode pupils face their fears at an outdoor adventure centre. The focus of the trip is for the young people to have an opportunity to challenge their disability physically, and abseiling in a wheelchair epitomises this experience

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox invites actress and writer Meera Syal, rapper Guvna B, comedian Dave Gorman and newsreader Babita Sharma to discuss the books that take them back to their youth, the books that transport them to far-flung places and the books that make them feel positive about the world. They also discuss this week's book group pick, Love in Colour by Bolu Babalola, as well as Babita's book The Corner Shop

programme.title: Snow Cats and Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan joins pioneering biologist Dr Victor Lukarevskiy in Russia to attempt to rehabilitate two pairs of lynx held captive for years. Two of these cats, Koshka and Miass, were born in the wild, but have been locked in tiny steel cages for years by a fur trapper, while their Bryansk and Dasha have never seen the wilderness, and Dr Lukarevskiy wants to give these lynx a new life in the wild. Will they have the instincts needed to survive in the wild? And after years in human captivity, will they make it?

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The Regency rea is often regarded as genteel and well-ordered, with beautiful buildings, Jane Austen romance and red-coated officers defeating Napoleon at Waterloo. However, historian Lucy Worsley digs behind the facade of Georgian elegance to reveal the fibs that helped conceal a darker side to the Regency and suppressed rebellion in an age of revolution. She discovers that to quell rebellion, monarchy and government relied on spin, secrets and lies

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Contributors including Bonnie Tyler, Tracie Young, Marilyn and members of New Order, Kajagoogoo, Haysi Fantayzee, Spandau Ballet, the Smiths, Flying Pickets and Thompson Twins reflect on an era when the mood in the charts was escapism and musical reinvention. It was a difficult time for the Top of the Pops itself, as the rise of the video started to threaten its unique appeal - getting the day's top artists into the studio. This forced producer Michael Hurll to experiment with presenters and features - including dispensing with dance troupe Zoo

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories, presented by Faisal Islam

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the second day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia. Francesco Molinari and Brooks Koepka shared the lead at the end of this stage last year, with Tiger Woods one stroke behind as he bid to win the title for the fifth time in his career

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part one of two. Starbuck, Boomer and Helo return to the Astral Queen, where Roslin forces them on a desperate mission to find the Tomb of Athena. Adama appoints an inexperienced replacement for his son, who proves inadequate in almost everything he's asked to do - endangering the lives of the rest of the fleet in the process. Edward James Olmos stars

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part two of two. Adama reassembles the fleet and follows President Roslin to the surface of Kobol, where she searches for the tomb of Athena that will lead them to Earth. The estranged groups make peace and reunite to continue the expedition, but their destination holds an unusual surprise. Meanwhile, Meier resorts to drastic measures to ensure Zarek's rise to power

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Jane Corbin finds out how Liverpool coped with restrictions due to Covid-19 infection rates, hearing from residents worried about the second wave, business owners pushed to the brink of despair, and those who say that living under tier three rules just isn't possible

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A 75-year-old patient has an enlarged aorta, the main vessel for carrying oxygenated blood to the body. Her condition could cause her to die at any time - but treating it also poses a risk to her life. At Addenbrooke's hospital, a patient with a genetic condition that makes her susceptible to cancer undergoes treatment to remove a polyp that has the potential to turn into cancer

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood share love-themed stories from the natural world, including mayflies speed-dating and a kissing bug that loves humans

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his friends hitch a ride on a bamboo railway in Cambodia and release rescued baby turtles into the ocean in Indonesia

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Community on Solid Ground is a youth centre in Whalley Range. Before its current management took over the building the area didn't offer much for the youngsters living there and anti-social behaviour was very common. That has dropped by 73 per cent since Community on Solid Ground came along, the kids can hang out, play sports and have a place to go that means they aren't hanging around the streets. Sadly the facilities are very tired and they don't have areas they can play their beloved sport on, so the Dengineers are going to transform their outside space into a sports arena worthy of the Olympics. Presented by Mark Wright and Lauren Layfield

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Richie Driss helps create a 3D book for children who are blind or partially sighted and Mwaksy Mudenda attempts British Sign Language. Plus, exclusive studio performances

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve investigates how weather could prove dangerous to dinosaurs, and recreates the asteroid that wiped the prehistoric creatures out

programme.title: The Big Life Fix with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Pollyanna lost a leg in an accident when she was two. Despite her challenges, she dreams of being a ballerina but her false leg does not allow her to go up onto her toes. Yusuf Mohammed takes up the challenge to build her a new prosthetic with the required movement in the foot. Also, Akram Hussein tries to come up with a solution for a 73-year-old deaf man whose nerve endings are too damaged to have a hearing aid, and materials expert Zoe Laughlin helps a man who is paralysed from the chest down find a way of cooking for his family

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present more battles in the arena. A team of engineering students has created a robot that is able to move easily in any direction, which they send into battle against a group of holiday camp staff and a father and son duo whose machine is equipped with huge piercing claws

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: First of a two-part programme catching up with zookeeper Giles Clark and the Sumatran tiger cubs he was hand-rearing in his home in the suburbs of Queensland. Spot and Stripe are approaching their first birthdays and Giles has his hands full guiding them from adolescence into adulthood, while Spot faces uncertainty about his eyesight and his relationship with Stripe. The Clarks also make a big decision to safeguard the cub's wild cousins, with Giles, his wife Kerri and son Kynan embarking on a road trip through Indonesia with the aim of helping to protect endangered species

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Following the stories of puppies and their owners, including border collie Meg, who has her work cut out for her as she trains to be a sheepdog. Cameras also follow great dane Shelby as he joins a young family which already includes Mr Cooper, a three-year-old dachshund

programme.title: Live Women's International Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v England (Kick-off 1.15pm). All the action from the autumn international at a venue to be confirmed, This is the first of two contests between the sides in successive weeks, with the teams set to meet at Twickenham next weekend. The last contest involving the nations came in February, when England prevailed 19-13 at Stade du Hameau in the opening match of the Six Nations

programme.title: Easter Parade (1948)
programme.desc: A dancer abandoned by his usual partner trains a chorus girl as a replacement. The new pairing is a great success but, although he strives to keep their relationship strictly professional, he begins to fall head over heels in love with her. Musical, with Fred Astaire, Judy Garland, Peter Lawford and Ann Miller. Songs include A Couple of Swells, Stepping Out with My Baby and Shaking the Blues Away

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Michael Baggott and Anita Manning are at the RAF Museum in Hendon, north London, where items include a pair of beaded gloves and an amber necklace. Presenter Paul Martin explores the history of one of Britain's few remaining Palladian houses

programme.title: Saving Britain's Pubs with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: For the past 20 years, pubs have been closing down at an alarming rate. Concerned by a crisis in the industry he loves, Tom Kerridge sets out on a mission to revive struggling pubs. He begins at the White Hart in Chilsworthy, Cornwall, where a 300-year-old freehouse, the only pub in the village, is in danger of closing

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring and the platoon are put in charge of guarding a stretch of telephone line that is vital to the war effort. During the night, there is an air raid and the men wake the next morning to find an unexploded bomb has caught in the wires. Comedy first shown in 1977, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Clive Dunn

programme.title: Mozart's Requiem from ENO
programme.desc: Mark Wigglesworth conducts the chorus and orchestra of the English National Opera and a stellar line-up of soloists - Elizabeth Llewellyn (soprano), Sarah Connolly (mezzo), Ed Lyon (tenor) and Gerald Finley (bass) - in Mozart's unfinished final work. Danielle De Niese presents the concert, recorded specially for a lockdown TV audience on the stage of the Coliseum in Covent Garden

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank's Monuments of Remembrance
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank reveals the extraordinary stories behind First World War cemeteries and memorials, including those at the Somme and Gallipoli,. He also explores the idea behind the creation of the Commonwealth War Graves Commission

programme.title: Journey's End (2017)
programme.desc: France, March 1918. Raleigh, a naive and idealistic young officer, arrives at the Western Front and asks to be posted to the company of his old school friend Captain Stanhope. He joins a trench full of fear, as the men await their grim fate while trying to cope with the daily pressures of the war. Adaptation of the play by RC Sherriff, starring Sam Claflin, Paul Bettany, Asa Butterfield, Toby Jones and Stephen Graham

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the third day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia. Italy's Francesco Molinari held the lead on 13-under at the end of this stage last year, with Tony Finau and Tiger Woods two strokes behind Brooks Koepka a further shot back. With commentary by Peter Allis, Ken Brown, Andrew Cotter and Iain Carter

programme.title: The Manchurian Candidate (1962)
programme.desc: A distinguished Korean War veteran is brainwashed into becoming a remote-controlled killing machine. When he returns to America a hero, an old friend begins to suspect something is terribly wrong. Political thriller, starring Laurence Harvey, Frank Sinatra, Janet Leigh and Angela Lansbury

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter Q, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter R, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith visit Cornwall, heading to Falmouth for the start of the oyster season, where successful hatching is down to the moon. They also discover how old traditions are being used in modern ways, from boatbuilding to using horses to manage an ancient landscape, while Charlotte helps out in the exotic gardens of Trelissick. Adam Henson finds out why British turkey farmers could be in for a tough time this Christmas.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits the Abriachan Forest by Loch Ness, one of the first community woodlands in Scotland. Euan McIlwraith is in Moray to investigate the worrying increase in incidents of hare coursing and Arlene Stuart finds out why there is a problem with the provision of public toilets across the country

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Looking at the physical and mental benefits of gardening, including a visit to a unique community growing project in Dundee combating poverty and giving escape from addiction

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: Born in 1962 to Nigerian parents studying medicine in London, Kemi Nevins now runs a successful cafe in Abercynon serving up delicious food. However, none of that she offers reflects her Nigerian background. Now Kemi's children are adults themselves, they would like to know more about their heritage, so the Welsh-Italian cook visits Kemi to help her reconnect to her roots through her love of food

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate British beef, using it in a suet pudding and making oxtail soup. They visit a farmer who is focusing on the future with his wagyu cattle, discover the history of the much-maligned dish tripe and learn how to roast a whole ox

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Setting out on his horticultural tour, Monty Don finds a tasteful blend of ancient and modern landscaping in the antiquated water gardens of Mexico City, and the post-modern surreal fantasies of Luis Barragan in the Mexican rainforest. On the Caribbean island of Cuba, the intrepid gardener discovers a crumbling urban landscape playing host to a budding natural revolution

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of day one from The O2 in London, where the opening singles round-robin match takes place. The season-ending tournament will see the top eight available singles players in the world compete for this prestigious title, which was won last year by Stefanos Tsitsipas when he defeated Dominic Thiem in the final

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Will Axon assess items at Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, including a Busby hat and a Worcester coffee set. Presenter Paul Martin explores the Battle of Edgehill

programme.title: 633 Squadron (1964)
programme.desc: British airmen embark on a daring mission to destroy a heavily fortified German rocket-fuel plant in Norway, and to ensure success the RAF pilots have to bomb an overhanging cliff to collapse it onto the building. The plan threatens to unravel, however, when a Norwegian freedom fighter plotting a land attack to coincide with the air strike is captured by the Nazis. Second World War drama, starring Cliff Robertson, George Chakiris, Donald Houston and Harry Andrews

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The RNLI volunteers at Dunmore East in the Irish Sea are called to a motorboat stranded several miles out and taking on water, a man is caught under a pier in the Thames and two boys in north Wales get caught in a treacherous current chasing an errant football

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: An inventor with a passion for pets hopes Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Jenny Campbell and Tej Lalvani will appreciate his cat whistle, while an ice-cream entrepreneur plans to tempt the multi-millionaires to invest in her coconut-based desserts. Plus, a carpenter brings the outside indoors for some fun and games

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his two-part journey, Simon Reeve investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country. He discovers how the region's farmers are adopting new, less intensive approaches that are both more profitable and environmentally friendly, and reveals how reintroducing beavers to the uplands could tackle the problem of widespread flooding. He also encounters some of the county's iconic wildlife, including grey seals that live in constant risk of getting caught up in discarded fishing nets

programme.title: The Trials of Oscar Pistorius
programme.desc: Documentary series focusing on the South African former Paralympic star, who was convicted for the murder of his girlfriend Reeva Steenkamp on Valentine's Day in 2013. A month after the shooting, Oscar Pistorius was released on bail, and moved into his uncle's compound. His legal team hired a forensic expert to recreate his version of events

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the fourth and final day of the Major, which took place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia. Tiger Woods won this title for the first time since 2005 last year, finishing one stroke clear of American compatriots Dustin Johnson, Brooks Koepka and Xander Schauffele. With commentary by Peter Allis, Ken Brown, Andrew Cotter and Iain Carter

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: The mystery surrounding Peter's appearance in Ireland is revealed in an episode exploring the events of the past four months. Nathan, Niki and DL's back stories are recalled, while Maya and Alejandro go on the run from the authorities. Milo Ventimiglia, Adrian Pasdar and Dania Ramirez star

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Noah Bennet causes an argument with Claire when he prepares to move his family because the Company has found them. Matt attempts to master his new mental abilities, while Hiro returns to the present to find out his father is dead - so he travels back in time to prevent his murder. Masi Oka and Hayden Panettiere star

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric wakes up from his coma to find the world in the middle of the Covid-19 pandemic - and that the ongoing crisis threatens his chance of surviving his recent brain surgery. Sacha's colleagues are concerned by how he is taking Essie's death, but he turns his back on them in favour of a stranger, while Cameron struggles to prove that he can be a hero

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox invites Laura Whitmore, Anita Rani, Russell Kane and Alan Davies to discuss the books that take them back to their youth, the books that transport them to far-flung places and the books that make them feel positive about the world. They also talk about this week's book group picks - Small Pleasures by Clare Chambers and Alan Davies' new book Just Ignore Him

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Live Lounge Show
programme.desc: Clara Amfo peeks behind the scenes of Radio 1's Live Lounge, the twice-weekly feature attracting an array of some of the biggest and best pop artists on the planet. Here, she features Miley Cyrus, Jorja Smith and Little Mix

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first aired on March 15, with performances by artists including Inspiral Carpets, Primal Scream, Candy Flip, New Kids on the Block, the Mission, the B-52's, Beats International featuring Lindy Layton and Jive Bunny and the Mastermixers

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on March 22, with performances by artists including Orbital, Snap!, Big Fun, Erasure, 49ers, David A Stewart featuring Candy Dulfer, Jimmy Somerville and Beats International featuring Lindy Layton

programme.title: Ella Fitzgerald: Just One of Those Things
programme.desc: The life and times of the jazz singer, beginning with a 1934 talent contest at the Apollo Theatre in Harlem, and following her next five decades, reflecting the passions and troubles of the times in which she lived in her life and her music. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo, with contributions from Laura Mvula, Ray Brown Jr, Tony Bennett, Johnny Mathis, Jamie Cullum, Cleo Laine and Smokey Robinson

programme.title: Ella Fitzgerald Sings
programme.desc: A performance from 1965 by the jazz singer accompanied by the Johnny Spence Orchestra, including big band versions of Don't Rain on My Parade and Mack the Knife

programme.title: Jazz Divas Gold
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances by acclaimed female jazz artists from shows including Not So Much a Programme - More a Way of Life, Morecambe and Wise, Parkinson and Show of the Week. Featuring Ella Fitzgerald, Peggy Lee, Blossom Dearie, Nina Simone, Cleo Laine, Marion Montgomery, Betty Carter, Sarah Vaughan, Eartha Kitt and Amy Winehouse

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: After his meteoric rise to fame in the '90s, Seal has had a remarkable career and has released a string of hits that included Killer, Crazy and Kiss from a Rose. Jo Whiley introduces an evening of old songs and new in this specially shot concert from 2015 for BBC Radio 2

programme.title: The Live Lounge Show
programme.desc: Clara Amfo peeks behind the scenes of Radio 1's Live Lounge, the twice-weekly feature attracting an array of some of the biggest and best pop artists on the planet. Here, she features Miley Cyrus, Jorja Smith and Little Mix

programme.title: Ella Fitzgerald: Just One of Those Things
programme.desc: The life and times of the jazz singer, beginning with a 1934 talent contest at the Apollo Theatre in Harlem, and following her next five decades, reflecting the passions and troubles of the times in which she lived in her life and her music. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo, with contributions from Laura Mvula, Ray Brown Jr, Tony Bennett, Johnny Mathis, Jamie Cullum, Cleo Laine and Smokey Robinson

programme.title: Francesco's Italy Top to Toe
programme.desc: Author and architect Francesco da Mosto presents a guide to his native Italy. He starts in the North, where he views Leonardo's Last Supper in Milan and explores one of the world's oldest universities. He also visits Florence, the birthplace of Renaissance art, and Verona, the setting for Romeo and Juliet, before going underwater to see the statue of Christ off the coast of Portofino

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: From Cape Horn, Michael follows the route to Chile taken by Charles Darwin on HMS Beagle and finds a country of staggering beauty. He then journeys across the Andes by single-carriage train to La Paz in Bolivia, which, at two miles above sea level, is the highest capital city in the world

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: Neel visits Melanie's family to investigate the trafficking of babies, but Rolf soon realises that she could be in danger. Meanwhile, Julita refuse to give up on her stolen child. In Danish, English, French and Polish

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: With the people behind the adoption network in custody, Minna is reunited with her mother. Meanwhile, Rolf gets a new lead in the search for his missing daughter. In Danish, English, French and Polish

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first aired on March 15, with performances by artists including Inspiral Carpets, Primal Scream, Candy Flip, New Kids on the Block, the Mission, the B-52's, Beats International featuring Lindy Layton and Jive Bunny and the Mastermixers

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on March 22, with performances by artists including Orbital, Snap!, Big Fun, Erasure, 49ers, David A Stewart featuring Candy Dulfer, Jimmy Somerville and Beats International featuring Lindy Layton

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The body of a woman is retrieved close to the bridge between Denmark and Sweden. It is found to be that of the Head of the Danish Immigration Board, whose staff had recently been filmed celebrating the success of a case deporting an Iranian homosexual who could face execution on his return. Since Saga has been imprisoned for the murder of her mother, Henrik investigates with his partner Jonas. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Thure Lindhardt

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Saga wants to return to work following her traumatic assault, but while Henrik wants to have her on the investigation, Lillian takes some convincing. Saga remains obsessed with finding Henrik's daughters, while he wonders if he will have to give in to the idea that they are no longer alive in order to finally move on. The investigation into Margrethe Thormod's murder continues, with suspicion falling on a radical left wing group and a refugee who has gone missing after being rejected for asylum. In Danish and Swedish

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: From Cape Horn, Michael follows the route to Chile taken by Charles Darwin on HMS Beagle and finds a country of staggering beauty. He then journeys across the Andes by single-carriage train to La Paz in Bolivia, which, at two miles above sea level, is the highest capital city in the world

programme.title: Francesco's Italy Top to Toe
programme.desc: Author and architect Francesco da Mosto presents a guide to his native Italy. He starts in the North, where he views Leonardo's Last Supper in Milan and explores one of the world's oldest universities. He also visits Florence, the birthplace of Renaissance art, and Verona, the setting for Romeo and Juliet, before going underwater to see the statue of Christ off the coast of Portofino

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the wildlife of the US state, including footage of manatees, baby alligators and miniature deer living in Florida's pine forests, coral reefs and famous Everglades wetlands. The programme considers the dangers facing the animal population, including natural disasters like wildfires, flooding and hurricanes, as well as the pressure of a growing human presence in their territory

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob explores the musical heritage of New Orleans, and considers what the future holds for the city in the aftermath of Hurricane Katrina. He talks to local performers Aaron Neville, Irma Thomas and Clarence Henry, who are determined to return to their home town, while Paul McCartney, Jools Holland, Dr John and Elvis Costello discuss how the city's musicians have inspired their work

programme.title: Ronnie's: Ronnie Scott & His World-Famous Jazz Club
programme.desc: The history of Ronnie Scott's Jazz Club, eponymously named after the late tenor saxophonist who founded the establishment with business partner Pete King. It opened in London's Soho in October 1959, inspired by the vibrant post-war jazz venues in New York. Since then, Ronnie's has consistently played host to many of the world's greatest jazz legends, including Chet Baker, Count Basie, Miles Davies, Ella Fitzgerald, Buddy Rich and Nina Simone who are all featured in the film. Contributors include Ronnie's friends, family and the music elite, including Chris Blackwell, Quincy Jones and Sonny Rollins

programme.title: Ella Fitzgerald at Ronnie Scott's
programme.desc: A 1974 performance at the London club by the American jazz singer, including Sweet Georgia Brown, It Don't Mean a Thing (If It Ain't Got That Swing) and Swingin' the Blues

programme.title: Jazz Horns Gold
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances by jazz artists from shows including Jazz 625, The Late Show, Later - with Jools Holland and Crackerjack. Featuring Dizzy Gillespie, Coleman Hawkins, Stan Getz, Rahsaan Roland Kirk, Wynton Marsalis, Acker Bilk, John Dankworth, Courtney Pine, John Surman and Andy Hamilton. Plus, the sounds of jazz disciples of the 21st century such as Joshua Redman and Hypnotic Brass Ensemble

programme.title: 21st Century Mythologies with Richard Clay
programme.desc: The art historian explores some of the everyday myths of 21st-century popular culture, revealing the hidden meanings in everything from money, Wi-fi and race to the Madonna. Along the way, he meets avant-garde artists including Clet Abraham, Ingrid Burrington, Molly Soda and Rene Matic, and looks at semiotics, the study of signs and symbols that provides an analytical toolkit to helps navigate advertising and its demands for attention

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob explores the musical heritage of New Orleans, and considers what the future holds for the city in the aftermath of Hurricane Katrina. Twelve-year-old Chloe leads a lonely existence - her mother is too busy with her political ambitions to take any interest in her daughter, her hen-pecked dad is hiding a secret of his own and she cannot hope to compete with her 'perfect' younger sister - to say nothing of the nasty girls who make her school life a misery.
