Back on the mainland, Jules travels to south-west Wales to meet a husband and wife who craved a sizable Welsh farmhouse, but who have found that a home is about so much more than bricks and mortar

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Presenter Jay Blades enlists the help of designer Sarah Peterson to give two side tables a stylish new look, while lighting expert Duncan McKean attempts to give two old record players a radical overhaul. For his own project, Jay attempts to make some money for nothing from two farmhouse stools. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The open road brings Charles Hanson and James Braxton - and their classic cars - to Hampshire. Along the way, Charles struggles with the art of signwriting and James meets a little owl. James is also drawn to an exotic alcohol container, while Charles' eye lands on an unusual pocket watch stand. The duo then head to the final auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which five pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: A jam-packed evening of socially distanced entertainment, with exclusive sketches, exciting performances, music, dance and a few surprises. Mel Giedroyc is joined by Alex Scott, Chris Ramsey and Stephen Mangan, who will all be co-hosting the fund-raising telethon for the first time. There are Strictly Come Dancing, EastEnders and Doctor Who-related surprises in store, while Matt Baker, Ade Adepitan and the team of six young riders make their way to the finishing line after 332 miles around the Goodwood Estate as part of The One Show's 2020 Rickshaw Challenge. Beverley Knight and Shawn Mendes perform, while The Repair Shop team gives one very special girl a surprise to treasure for ever

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: A jam-packed evening of socially distanced entertainment, with exclusive sketches, exciting performances, music, dance and a few surprises. Mel Giedroyc is joined by Alex Scott, Chris Ramsey and Stephen Mangan, who will all be co-hosting the fund-raising telethon for the first time. There are Strictly Come Dancing, EastEnders and Doctor Who-related surprises in store, while Matt Baker, Ade Adepitan and the team of six young riders make their way to the finishing line after 332 miles around the Goodwood Estate as part of The One Show's 2020 Rickshaw Challenge. Beverley Knight and Shawn Mendes perform, while The Repair Shop team gives one very special girl a surprise to treasure for ever

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The sceptical comedian is on the hunt for something he doesn't think exists. Teaming up with a band of ghost hunters, Rhod is expected to host a paranormal evening for the public. Desperate to meet those beyond the grave, he tries to put himself in the scariest situations he can find

programme.title: Dark Land: Hunting the Killers
programme.desc: A quiet Cardiff community was devastated in 1959 by the abduction of six-year-old Carol Ann Stephens, last seen getting into a car with a mysterious, dark-haired stranger. A nationwide recovery effort was launched, but two weeks later, Carol Ann's body was found in a brook, 60 miles from where she'd been taken. Her killer was never caught. The team comes across new evidence that could finally put this case to rest

programme.title: A Simple Favour (2018)
programme.desc: Stephanie Smothers, a widowed single mother who runs a crafts and recipes vlog, becomes friends with Emily Nelson, a public relations director for a fashion company. Emily has a work crisis and asks Stephanie to babysit her son, but later goes missing amid suggestions from colleagues she has jetted off to Miami. Feeling perplexed, Stephanie attempts to get to the bottom of her friend's mysterious actions. Paul Feig's comedy thriller based on Darcey Bell's novel, starring Anna Kendrick, Blake Lively and Henry Golding

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ching-He Huang and Vivek Singh, and special guest Melanie C. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares the rhythms and rituals of her kitchen and reveals the inspiration behind many of her recipes. She begins with her take on bhorta, a dish of fried and mashed vegetables mainly associated with Bangladesh, to which she introduces a very British - but very familiar - ingredient. After creating a warm, spicy and aromatic dish of lamb shanks with wide noodles, Nigella uses very ripe bananas to make a rich and gooey chocolate tahini pudding, and then goes on to create a warming curry with a surprising ingredient

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker look ahead to the weekend's UEFA Nations League fixtures, including Belgium v England, Wales v Republic of Ireland, Slovakia v Scotland and Austria v Northern Ireland

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in Sutton Coldfield, Kent and Ipswich, then talk to the buyers about their plans

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams acquire antiques in Brighton with the help of experts Danny Sebastian and John Cameron, then take their items to auction in Bllingshurst. Presenter Charlie Ross also takes in the Royal Pavilion and discovers its connection to famous milliner Stephen Jones

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple find a property in the North Yorkshire countryside on their £400,000 budget, and he also traverses Stean Gorge on a series of high-wire cables

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items reclaimed from Witley Recycling Centre, including a cooker hood and a collection of old jars

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, David and Harry compete to design an eco-friendly garden in Whitchurch, Hampshire, which includes a space for entertaining and tackles a dangerous two-metre drop

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway heads into the wilds of Snowdonia with disabled rambler Terry Taylor as they explore an old Roman road leading across the Carneddau Mountains. He then goes south to Skomer Island off Pembrokeshire where, guided by warden Bee Buche, he gets a close-up view of the puffin population before staying up late to see the reclusive Manx shearwater

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Lee Thompson, Pauline Black, Steve Brown, Barbara Dickson, Kimberly Wyatt, Cathy Dennis, Heather Small and Steve Hogarth

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a special celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, as a host of famous faces compete to win £10,000 for a charity of their choice. The contestants are Strictly Come Dancing professional dancers AJ Pritchard and his brother Curtis, journalist and newsreader Kate Silverton, TV presenter Anneka Rice, resident This Morning doctor Dr Ranj Singh, and Radio 1 DJ Dev Griffin

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Last week, it seemed the force was not with Jason and Luba as they performed their Star Wars paso doble, and they became the second couple to be voted off the show. Sadly, Nicola and Katya have also dropped out of the competition after Katya tested positive for Covid-19. That leaves nine couples to take the dance floor, now more aware than ever that as they learn their routines and face the judges, they have one more potential hurdle that could appear at any time. Presented by Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fifth episode, married couple Dan and Alison from Swansea face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund Blackadder, conniving courtier to Elizabeth I, takes in a spirited lad as his manservant - unaware the fellow is actually a woman out to make her fortune. Classic comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson, Miranda Richardson and Stephen Fry, with a guest appearance by Rik Mayall as Edmund's best friend Lord Flashheart

programme.title: Don't Take Me Home
programme.desc: A look at how Wales fared at Euro 2016, the first time they had qualified for a major international football tournament since the 1958 World Cup. Featuring match highlights and interviews with the players and coaching staff.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: A secretive phone call from a solicitor to Mrs Brown leaves her children suspecting one of them may be adopted, while Father Quinn returns from rehab and asks Agnes to help out at his annual garden party - although as she proceeds to drive him crazy with a smartphone, his recovery may be short-lived. Meanwhile, Cathy receives a blast from the past, Rory and Dino have a bust-up, and Mark and Betty deliver bad news - they are leaving for Australia right away. Domestic comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a round-up of the latest fixtures, including Tennessee Titans v Indianapolis Colts

programme.title: Operation Avalanche (2016)
programme.desc: Two young CIA agents are sent to NASA, under the false pretence of making a documentary, to investigate reports of a mole. Once there, though, they become entangled in a much bigger conspiracy involving the Apollo 11 mission and the filming techniques of director Stanley Kubrick. Thriller, starring Matt Johnson, Owen Williams and Andrew Appelle

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow projects involving a listed Victorian lodge in Plaistow, London, an end terrace in Liverpool and a former pub in Blackburn

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks offer advice to two teams at the Highland Centre in Edinburgh, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to a camera obscura in the city

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is back on home turf in north Wales as he takes a journey exploring how his faith has shaped his life and his singing, catching up with composer Howard Goodall and the Rev Canon Mark Oakley along the way. Aled sings a couple of songs that are special to him and picks his favourite hymns for the programme, including Guide Me, O Thou Great Jehovah and How Great Thou Art

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades rescues three items about to be dumped at an Altrincham tip, with the experts transforming a table, a record player and a farmhouse chair

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple search for a country home close to the coast in Hampshire. While in the area, she also pays a visit the Mary Rose Museum

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway heads to the banks of the Menai Strait on a walk between the Britannia Bridge and the Menai Suspension Bridge. His guides are local Women's Institute members, which seems apt as it was on Anglesey that Britain's first ever WI meeting was held over a hundred years ago. Later, Derek treks between two captivating castles at Caerphilly and Castell Coch, and even gets a chance to dress up as a medieval knight

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Highlights of the night's fundraising activities, which were hosted by Kiri Pritchard-McLean, including a performance by singer-songwriter Kizzy Crawford

programme.title: Coal House
programme.desc: The three families reflect on their experiences of life as it was in the mining communities of the 1920s, talking about the bonds of friendship they have formed as well as the fun and hardship they have been through

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich assist a woman in Hampshire, whose front garden is out of control, with lots of messy shrubs and a front door the postman cannot find. They also help animal lover Sue in Lymington, who would like to share her garden with the hedgehogs that live in the neighbourhood

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison explores the powers of the great outdoors to provide a tonic in troubled times as she immerses herself in nature, from forest bathing to wild swimming. There are also films from the Countryfile archives about the health-giving prowess of the UK's green havens, including John Craven finding out how nature helped heal wartime wounds, and Anita Rani visiting a special farm giving respite to refugees

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before the judges decide who will be the third pair to leave the contest. With music by the Vamps

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra crosses into Will's world and finds it to be very different to her own, and after encountering the enigmatic Charles Latrom, the pair head to find a scholar to help get answers about Dust. Will is shocked to discover he has grandparents, but quickly realises he can't trust them, while a calculating Mrs Coulter pushes MacPhail to act after Graves' strong performance during the interrogation of a witch consul

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: The first episode of Steve McQueen's drama anthology based on the real-life experiences of London's West Indian community from the late-1960s to the mid-1980s. The film tells the story of the Old Bailey trial of Frank Crichlow, the owner of West Indian restaurant Mangrove in London's Notting Hill, who in 1970 was one of nine men and women wrongly arrested and charged with incitement to riot and affray after a protest about racial discrimination by the police. Starring Shaun Parkes, Letitia Wright and Malachi Kirby

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester United v Manchester City and Aston Villa v Birmingham City

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of terraced houses in Walthamstow in London and Dover in Kent, and a three-bedroom semi in the Essex town of Epping

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Two sisters who tried to cheat their insurers following a car crash. Plus, a suspiciously unlucky computer user caught out after making multiple claims for lost laptops. Presented by Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: The emergency services attend a baby who has cut his throat on a glass. Plus, a woman is knocked to the ground and trampled after a dog spooks horses. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Coronavirus: Scotland's Response
programme.desc: All the latest developments as Scotland responds to the coronavirus pandemic, plus answers to the many questions you have during these unprecedented times

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

These creatures have a remarkable aptitude for communication to the point where they can interact with other species including octopi and work together to catch prey. Plus, footage of a vast spawning event involving several different species of fish, coral and invertebrates, and time-lapse photography of coral polyps growing and dying, creating a structure visible from space

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers get to grips with a specially designed pram for messy eating, a super-absorbent sponge that can soak up 12 times as much water as normal, and an anti-theft sports bag

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson returns to the Isle of Wight, the location of a previous property hunt, wanting to discover whether a couple he helped succeeded in finding a balance of home and business potential in their new setting. Back on the mainland, Jules travels to south-west Wales to meet a husband and wife who craved a sizable Welsh farmhouse, but who have found that a home is about so much more than bricks and mortar

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Presenter Jay Blades enlists the help of designer Sarah Peterson to give two side tables a stylish new look, while lighting expert Duncan McKean attempts to give two old record players a radical overhaul. For his own project, Jay attempts to make some money for nothing from two farmhouse stools. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The open road brings Charles Hanson and James Braxton - and their classic cars - to Hampshire. Along the way, Charles struggles with the art of signwriting and James meets a little owl. James is also drawn to an exotic alcohol container, while Charles' eye lands on an unusual pocket watch stand. The duo then head to the final auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which five pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: A jam-packed evening of socially distanced entertainment, with exclusive sketches, exciting performances, music, dance and a few surprises. Mel Giedroyc is joined by Alex Scott, Chris Ramsey and Stephen Mangan, who will all be co-hosting the fund-raising telethon for the first time. There are Strictly Come Dancing, EastEnders and Doctor Who-related surprises in store, while Matt Baker, Ade Adepitan and the team of six young riders make their way to the finishing line after 332 miles around the Goodwood Estate as part of The One Show's 2020 Rickshaw Challenge. Beverley Knight and Shawn Mendes perform, while The Repair Shop team gives one very special girl a surprise to treasure for ever

programme.title: A Simple Favour (2018)
programme.desc: Stephanie Smothers, a widowed single mother who runs a crafts and recipes vlog, becomes friends with Emily Nelson, a public relations director for a fashion company. Emily has a work crisis and asks Stephanie to babysit her son, but later goes missing amid suggestions from colleagues she has jetted off to Miami. Feeling perplexed, Stephanie attempts to get to the bottom of her friend's mysterious actions. Paul Feig's comedy thriller based on Darcey Bell's novel, starring Anna Kendrick, Blake Lively and Henry Golding

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ching-He Huang and Vivek Singh, and special guest Melanie C. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares the rhythms and rituals of her kitchen and reveals the inspiration behind many of her recipes. She begins with her take on bhorta, a dish of fried and mashed vegetables mainly associated with Bangladesh, to which she introduces a very British - but very familiar - ingredient. After creating a warm, spicy and aromatic dish of lamb shanks with wide noodles, Nigella uses very ripe bananas to make a rich and gooey chocolate tahini pudding, and then goes on to create a warming curry with a surprising ingredient

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker look ahead to the weekend's UEFA Nations League fixtures, including Belgium v England, Wales v Republic of Ireland, Slovakia v Scotland and Austria v Northern Ireland

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in Sutton Coldfield, Kent and Ipswich, then talk to the buyers about their plans

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams acquire antiques in Brighton with the help of experts Danny Sebastian and John Cameron, then take their items to auction in Bllingshurst. Presenter Charlie Ross also takes in the Royal Pavilion and discovers its connection to famous milliner Stephen Jones

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple find a property in the North Yorkshire countryside on their £400,000 budget, and he also traverses Stean Gorge on a series of high-wire cables

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues a sideboard, a chaise longue and an overcoat bound for the tip, before she and two others restyle them in the hope of making a profit for the owners

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: A Letchworth couple receive a surprise £4,500 garden makeover as a thank you from their son - a challenge that Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers find difficult to resist

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Lee Thompson, Pauline Black, Steve Brown, Barbara Dickson, Kimberly Wyatt, Cathy Dennis, Heather Small and Steve Hogarth

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a special celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, as a host of famous faces compete to win £10,000 for a charity of their choice. The contestants are Strictly Come Dancing professional dancers AJ Pritchard and his brother Curtis, journalist and newsreader Kate Silverton, TV presenter Anneka Rice, resident This Morning doctor Dr Ranj Singh, and Radio 1 DJ Dev Griffin

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Last week, it seemed the force was not with Jason and Luba as they performed their Star Wars paso doble, and they became the second couple to be voted off the show. Sadly, Nicola and Katya have also dropped out of the competition after Katya tested positive for Covid-19. That leaves nine couples to take the dance floor, now more aware than ever that as they learn their routines and face the judges, they have one more potential hurdle that could appear at any time. Presented by Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fifth episode, married couple Dan and Alison from Swansea face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund Blackadder, conniving courtier to Elizabeth I, takes in a spirited lad as his manservant - unaware the fellow is actually a woman out to make her fortune. Classic comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson, Miranda Richardson and Stephen Fry, with a guest appearance by Rik Mayall as Edmund's best friend Lord Flashheart

programme.title: Ant-Man (2015)
programme.desc: A thief attempting to go straight is recruited by a scientist who provides him with a suit that gives him the power to shrink. He must use his new skills to carry out a special mission - helping the inventor to steal back his miniaturisation technology from a ruthless businessman who intends to turn it into a deadly weapon. Superhero adventure, starring Paul Rudd, Michael Douglas and Evangeline Lilly

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a round-up of the latest fixtures, including Tennessee Titans v Indianapolis Colts

programme.title: Operation Avalanche (2016)
programme.desc: Two young CIA agents are sent to NASA, under the false pretence of making a documentary, to investigate reports of a mole. Once there, though, they become entangled in a much bigger conspiracy involving the Apollo 11 mission and the filming techniques of director Stanley Kubrick. Thriller, starring Matt Johnson, Owen Williams and Andrew Appelle

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow projects involving a listed Victorian lodge in Plaistow, London, an end terrace in Liverpool and a former pub in Blackburn

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks offer advice to two teams at the Highland Centre in Edinburgh, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to a camera obscura in the city

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is back on home turf in north Wales as he takes a journey exploring how his faith has shaped his life and his singing, catching up with composer Howard Goodall and the Rev Canon Mark Oakley along the way. Aled sings a couple of songs that are special to him and picks his favourite hymns for the programme, including Guide Me, O Thou Great Jehovah and How Great Thou Art

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore enlists the talents of master carpenter Norman Wilkinson, designer Daniel Heath and lighting guru Guy Trench to transform four items she has salvaged from the nation's tips into top-end treasured pieces

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple search for a country home close to the coast in Hampshire. While in the area, she also pays a visit the Mary Rose Museum

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Holly Hamilton catches up with Northern Ireland's local fundraising, and Eamonn Holmes looks back over the last 40 years of Children in Need

programme.title: Life Is Magic
programme.desc: Highlights from the programme in which illusionist Joel M took his conjuring skills to parties and events around the country, helping to create life-changing celebrations

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live on coral reefs, including groupers, small predators that scientists believe are as intelligent as chimpanzees. These creatures have a remarkable aptitude for communication to the point where they can interact with other species including octopi and work together to catch prey. Plus, footage of a vast spawning event involving several different species of fish, coral and invertebrates, and time-lapse photography of coral polyps growing and dying, creating a structure visible from space

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison explores the powers of the great outdoors to provide a tonic in troubled times as she immerses herself in nature, from forest bathing to wild swimming. There are also films from the Countryfile archives about the health-giving prowess of the UK's green havens, including John Craven finding out how nature helped heal wartime wounds, and Anita Rani visiting a special farm giving respite to refugees

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before the judges decide who will be the third pair to leave the contest. With music by the Vamps

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra crosses into Will's world and finds it to be very different to her own, and after encountering the enigmatic Charles Latrom, the pair head to find a scholar to help get answers about Dust. Will is shocked to discover he has grandparents, but quickly realises he can't trust them, while a calculating Mrs Coulter pushes MacPhail to act after Graves' strong performance during the interrogation of a witch consul

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: The first episode of Steve McQueen's drama anthology based on the real-life experiences of London's West Indian community from the late-1960s to the mid-1980s. The film tells the story of the Old Bailey trial of Frank Crichlow, the owner of West Indian restaurant Mangrove in London's Notting Hill, who in 1970 was one of nine men and women wrongly arrested and charged with incitement to riot and affray after a protest about racial discrimination by the police. Starring Shaun Parkes, Letitia Wright and Malachi Kirby

programme.title: Match of the Day from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Austria v Northern Ireland. Stephen Watson presents action from the Group B1 encounter at Ernst Happel Stadion in Vienna. With analysis from Jim Magilton

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester United v Manchester City and Aston Villa v Birmingham City

programme.title: Ten Minute Rule Bill
programme.desc: The introduction to the House of Commons of a bill under the ten minute rule by Labour MP Kerry McCarthy on the regulation of supported housing, from Wednesday 11 November

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Proceedings in the House of Commons' second chamber, Westminster Hall, including debates on spectator attendance at football matches during the Covid-19 pandemic and on support for live events and weddings during the pandemic, from Monday 9 November.

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Coverage of the final day of the committee stage of the UK Internal Market Bill in the House of Lords, from Monday 9 November

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Coverage of the second reading debate in the House of Lords on the Covert Intelligence Sources (Criminal Conduct) Bill, from Wednesday 11 November

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Public Accounts select committee session on support for jobs during the Covid-19 pandemic with evidence from Sir Tom Scholar, permanent secretary at the Treasury as well as senior Treasury and HMRC officials, from Thursday 12 November

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: A mix of light-hearted gossip and serious analysis about the latest goings on from Westminster and Brussels, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Five years ago, an electrician called Ali Motamed was shot and killed in Almere, just outside Amsterdam. It looked like a professional hit job - but why would anyone want to assassinate a seemingly innocent electrician? In the hours which followed, his wife told police he wasn't really Ali Motamed at all - his real name was Mohammad Reza Kolahi Samadi, and he was a wanted man in Tehran. So could the Iranian regime have ordered his killing? And could it also be behind the strikingly similar murder of another Iranian in The Hague in 2017? Jiyar Gol follows a trail of destruction across Europe: from the killings in the Netherlands, to a bomb plot in Paris in 2018 to an attempted assassination in Denmark. It's a story of spies, lies and geopolitics, with a string of extraordinary twists. Could Iran have orchestrated assassinations and an attempted bombing on European soil?

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Five years ago, an electrician called Ali Motamed was shot and killed in Almere, just outside Amsterdam. It looked like a professional hit job - but why would anyone want to assassinate a seemingly innocent electrician? In the hours which followed, his wife told police he wasn't really Ali Motamed at all - his real name was Mohammad Reza Kolahi Samadi, and he was a wanted man in Tehran. So could the Iranian regime have ordered his killing? And could it also be behind the strikingly similar murder of another Iranian in The Hague in 2017? Jiyar Gol follows a trail of destruction across Europe: from the killings in the Netherlands, to a bomb plot in Paris in 2018 to an attempted assassination in Denmark. It's a story of spies, lies and geopolitics, with a string of extraordinary twists. Could Iran have orchestrated assassinations and an attempted bombing on European soil?

programme.title: Murchadh MacPharlain: Bard Mhealaboist
programme.desc: Award-winning documentary profiling Murdo MacFarlane, the Melbost Bard, the renowned Gaelic poet and songwriter from the island of Lewis, whose work was a catalyst for a whole new era of modern Gaelic music. Having composed poems and songs all his life, it was not until the advent of the tape recorder that Murdo, who could neither read nor write music, became known to a wider audience. The programme contains TV and radio archive recordings of Murdo in interviews, and also of him performing his work

programme.desc: The dead body of a teenage boy is found in a reservoir near a remote rural timber community. Vera and her team investigate, and discover that the boy had already been missing for three days prior to his death. However, the case is complicated by an apparent conspiracy of silence, as many of the townsfolk refuse to cooperate with the police investigation. A complex web of relationships between the victim's friends and family slowly emerges, with many dark secrets that could have driven someone to kill

A complex web of relationships between the victim's friends and family slowly emerges, with many dark secrets that could have driven someone to kill

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: While sitting through the high school talent show, Peter becomes enraptured by the violin performance of a student and convinces Cleveland, Joe and Quagmire to join him in forming a string quartet. programme.title: Family Guy

programme.desc: Junior writes a profile of his dad for a school project about his favourite American - but his idealised vision is shattered when Cleveland suffers an embarrassing injury after falling off the toilet. programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna is unhappy about her relationship with mother Dee Dee, so Cleveland tries to cheer her up by hiring an actress to pose as her mum - but when the real Dee Dee unexpectedly visits, he spots an opportunity for a genuine reconciliation. programme.desc: Peter hurts himself and a family of squirrels in a chainsaw accident, and a chiropractor fixes his injury by realigning his spine - and restoring him to his true height

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter helps Joe solve cases, but gets irritated when the cop takes all the credit for himself. Meanwhile, Brian and Chris look after Stewie when he gets injured playing football

programme.title: Shopping with Keith Lemon
programme.desc: This time, Keith heads to an adventure park with presenter Mark Wright, and takes TV star Caitlyn Jenner grocery shopping

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter opens up his own food truck business to avoid the healthy diet Lois is forcing him to eat, while Meg joins a roller derby team despite Chris thinking it is too dangerous

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter becomes jealous of Lois when she upstages him at a Red Sox fantasy baseball camp. Meanwhile, Stewie runs away from home when Meg is left to babysit him

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg takes advantage of Peter's temporary job as principal of her school to get even with her bullies, while Brian and Stewie open a bed and breakfast

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Hayley and Jeff move into a van and start living life on the open road. Francine and Steve are haunted by a ride share driver that they rated poorly

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan begins to question Christianity after Steve asks some uncomfortable questions about everything written in the Bible. Now facing a crisis of faith, the secret agent takes his family to South Korea to investigate reports of reinvented Noah's Ark

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland flirts with a co-worker, but Rallo and Junior catch his antics on camera and use them to create a music video that becomes hugely popular on the internet. A major record producer also takes an interest - only to fall in love with Donna

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: One of Rallo's pranks goes too far and leads to Junior getting kicked out of the scouts. A furious Cleveland decides he has finally had enough of his stepson's misbehaviour - and threatens to send him to a young offenders' institution if matters do not improve. Animated sitcom, with the voice of Mike Henry

programme.title: Hey Tracey!
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts as Jordan Davies, AJ Odudu, Wes Nelson, Bobby Mair, Harriet Kemsley and Hannah Cooper try to win £2,500 and a tiny vacuum cleaner for members of the public

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: A selection of some of the most incredible and peculiar record-breaking attempts, including the tallest human pyramid and the longest ramp jump with a caravan. Matt Edmondson narrates

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: The police arrive at Home Farm to investigate James' disappearance, and the whole village begin searching for him. Steve encourages Kim to tell the police about the blackmailer

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned and Roy worry when Jan does not come home. Andy's social worker asks Jack and Sarah to consider fostering the youngster again. Ned finds a very emotionally distraught Jan

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Bless This House

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida and Angie brace themselves for a visit by Robert's 'other mother'. But they're not prepared for following events

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dispirited by a wet English summer, the Larkins cross the Channel in the hope that the French way of life will have a beneficial effect on the family. David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones star

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family faces the unwanted arrival of a domineering aunt, while Larry's girlfriend also pays a visit - giving the unwelcome relative an opportunity to voice her disapproval of the whole family. Sven makes an offer that helps Louisa cope with adversity and Margo falls in love - but Gerry is more concerned with his newly acquired owlets

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A prisoner is broken out of Ashfordly prison to pilot a helicopter on a secret mission, and Debbie begins acting strangely when a familiar face returns to the village. Meanwhile, Rob is left distraught when a boy takes revenge for the death of his dog

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned and Roy wait to see Jan at the police station. Jack and Sarah keep up the pretence that they are still together in front of the social worker

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack uses the situation with an Andy as an excuse to move back home. Ned speaks to a psychiatrist about Jan. Steve catches Kim on the phone arranging to meet the blackmailer

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Audrey tells Gail that Roy will ruin the cafe. Alf warns Ken that he has had a complaint over his affair with Sue. Chris Collins asks Kevin for work at the garage

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fraser demands to know who told the police about the job, and Liz is terrified when he lays into her. Ken warns Sue that people are gossiping about them

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the deaths of a female pensioner and a vicar who were both killed during Broughton village's annual contest between men and women. The pair soon discover the preacher had a secret that could provide a motive - but when another body is found, they realise the answer to all the deaths lies in the history books. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The mysterious death of a former police commissioner forces a reluctant Morse to team up with old professional rival Chief Inspector Dawson. As they and Sergeant Lewis begin to investigate, a link is uncovered to the unsolved case of a girl murdered 18 years previously. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Kenneth Colley and Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Japp and Poirot investigate the murder of an actress's estranged husband, only to find in the theatrical world, where deception and play-acting are a way of life, nothing is quite what it seems. In time-honoured fashion, it initially looks as though the butler did it - but subsequent inquiries throw up a veritable rogues' gallery of equally likely suspects. Philip Jackson, David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker (My Family)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sally considers a job which will take her away from the family home. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Carry On Again Doctor (1969)
programme.desc: A disgraced surgeon sets up a clinic in the hope of promoting a supposedly miraculous slimming potion made from secret ingredients discovered on a remote tropical island. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Jim Dale, Barbara Windsor, Charles Hawtrey, Hattie Jacques, Pat Coombs and Joan Sims

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne leans on Steve during their first day in court, Paul is furious that Todd is working at Underworld, and Sean and Michael embark on a new adventure

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective is ordered to take a holiday for the benefit of his health, and with Hastings and Miss Lemon in tow, reluctantly heads for the tranquillity of Devon's coast, where his relaxation is soon ruined by the discovery of a socialite's murdered body on the beach. Several people had reason for wishing Arlena Stuart dead, but high on the list of suspects are her husband, teenage son and a troubled vicar. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson and Pauline Moran star

programme.title: Carry On Loving (1970)
programme.desc: A couple who run a dating agency find themselves inundated with customers, and end up arranging a selection of very unlikely matches - while also contending with their own marital difficulties. Comedy, starring Sid James, Hattie Jacques, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Terry Scott

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: While investigating the shooting of a council clerk, Barnaby and Jones discover he was an expert blackmailer. A phone number on the dead man's blotter sends the duo to the seaside in search of his latest targets - whose boat trip could conceal a motive for the killing. Meanwhile, Joyce is convinced a local farmer knows more than he is telling. Julia McKenzie and Grant Masters guest star, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The Midsomer Worthy amateur choir is thrown into panic when baritone Connor Simpson is killed on the eve of the prestigious regional Four Choirs competition. A birdwatcher is also murdered shortly after, and DCI Barnaby and DC Jones uncover bitter rivalry and tense marital relationships as they try to establish if there is a connection between the two cases. Peter Capaldi guest stars

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Elverton-cum-Latterly gather to hold a protest meeting about proposals for a supermarket in the centre of the village, but proceedings are brought to a halt by the discovery of the site adviser's body. Barnaby and Jones discover the victim had a penchant for acting out role-play scenarios with local women - but that is not the only secret the villagers are hiding. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Midsomer Malham resident is accused of battering her best friend to death, but Barnaby cannot help feeling the evidence against her does not add up. The detective enlists the help of a pensioner, who also happens to be one of the key witnesses, to find out what really happened on the night of the murder - and realises the elderly lady's life could be at risk. With John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Thelma Barlow

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A case from 13 years earlier returns to haunt Lewis when the evidence comes under review. Graham Lawrie was incarcerated in a secure psychiatric hospital after being found guilty of the murders of three police officers, but now the findings of the forensics lab have been called into question and an appeal has been launched. As Lawrie remains locked up ahead of his day in court, PC Mark Travis is lured to an isolated location with a hoax call and falls victim to a killer who uses the same weapon as that used in the original murders

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: Becky has moved in with her rich aunt Matilda, and seems to have a bright future ahead of her. However, war is brewing on the horizon, which threatens the fortunes of the scheming social climber and everyone she knows. Adaptation of William Makepeace Thackeray's novel, starring Olivia Cooke

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid believes he is in for a fortune when a friend mentions him in his will. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus.Leanne leans on Steve during their first day in court, Paul is furious that Todd is working at Underworld, and Sean and Michael embark on a new adventure

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Bear is exasperated with Paddy, Lydia is shocked at Nate's admission, and Vinny is uneasy with Mandy's idea

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the deaths of a female pensioner and a vicar who were both killed during Broughton village's annual contest between men and women. The pair soon discover the preacher had a secret that could provide a motive - but when another body is found, they realise the answer to all the deaths lies in the history books. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The owner of local country magazine Midsomer Life becomes a suspect when a man's body is discovered in woodland, and it is established the victim had been having an affair with his wife. As Barnaby and Jones delve into the case, another murder forces them to reassess their inquiries. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Simon Williams

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Two couples disappear in the tiny village of Monks Barton amid rumours of witchcraft and haunted woods. As Barnaby and Jones investigate, psychic Cyrus LeVanu clashes with cleric Wallace Stone over their different methods of 'healing' the woods, while other locals seem to be caught up in a stolen antiques racket. When a body is unearthed and the death toll rises, the detectives race to catch the killer by finding a connection between the missing couples and the thefts. Anton Lesser guest stars, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: The Best of Cannon and Ball
programme.desc: A look back at some of the best, funniest and most outrageous moments from the career of comedy variety duo Cannon and Ball.

programme.title: Carry On Camping (1969)
programme.desc: Two men lure their unsuspecting girlfriends to a nudist camp, with hopes of having a wild old time - but the joke is on the men when it turns out they have ended up at a traditional family campsite. Bawdy comedy, starring Sid James, Bernard Bresslaw, Kenneth Williams, Joan Sims and Barbara Windsor

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Young physiotherapist Lizzie Faulkner is shot dead while enjoying a holiday at a luxury country retreat. The resulting murder investigation unearths betrayal, guilt and the desire for atonement - as Vera moves quickly to avoid another tragedy, and Joe tries to make amends with the demons of his past. Crime drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, David Leon, Paul Ritter and Eva Quinn

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: The police hold a press conference to appeal for information about Jonjo, and Smilie hopes to take the opportunity to reassure the public that they have nothing to fear and prevent news of the human heart found by the lake from spreading further. However, his plans are disrupted by Blake, who reveals everything and shares his theory that the community is being terrorised by a serial killer. An unexpected discovery is made outside the village hall during a town meeting set up to try and identify the killer

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the Shropshire countryside along the border between England and Wales, taking to the air to survey the landscape in all its glory. He then embarks on his final challenge, which takes him from Ludlow to the engineering marvel that is Chirk Aqueduct and on to Church Stretton and the Llangollen Canal

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sally and Mike enter the junk market and use the garden to store their wares - until Sid puts his foot down. Comedy, starring Sally Geeson and Robin Stewart

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Betty asks her neighbours to babysit, but Sid is less than keen to help. Comedy, starring Sid James and Patsy Rowlands

With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the Shropshire countryside along the border between England and Wales, taking to the air to survey the landscape in all its glory. He then embarks on his final challenge, which takes him from Ludlow to the engineering marvel that is Chirk Aqueduct and on to Church Stretton and the Llangollen Canal

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sally and Mike enter the junk market and use the garden to store their wares - until Sid puts his foot down. Comedy, starring Sally Geeson and Robin Stewart

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Betty asks her neighbours to babysit, but Sid is less than keen to help. Comedy, starring Sid James and Patsy Rowlands

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Bear is exasperated with Paddy, Lydia is shocked at Nate's admission, and Vinny is uneasy with Mandy's idea

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: Joe Perkins (guest star Patrick Troughton) joins the men's ward, but his apparent lack of hygiene and dubious behavioural habits make him few friends - until Figgis reveals he is a rich landowner. Comedy, with James Bolam, Peter Bowles and Christopher Strauli

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar encounters a girl who claims to be Hitler's daughter, and helps her evade Neo-Nazis. Vintage adventure, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Part one of two. Robin and the outlaws leave Sherwood to defend a distant village from a band of phantom horsemen known as the Hounds of Lucifer, who terrorise the countryside on the orders of a powerful coven ruled by Morgwyn of Ravenscar. Guest starring Rula Lenska

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: When a deranged scientist creates a time machine, Steed and Mrs Peel show him the error of his ways. With Patrick Macnee, Diana Rigg, and guest star Peter Bowles

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry minds a boxer, but is less than impressed to discover the promoter is betting on his opponent to win the next bout. Arthur, meanwhile, switches bets. Alfred Marks, Paul Barber and Vicki Michelle guest star

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A former agent regresses to a time when he was captured and brain-washed by the KGB into killing four of his colleagues. After escaping from a mental hospital, he sets out to resume his mission, leaving Bodie and Doyle with the task of trying to protect them. Action drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: An armed gang kidnaps a telephone engineer and uses his expertise to remotely trigger break-in alarms in various banks. While police are diverted to the affected premises, the crooks target other banks. Regan is offended when a telecommunications expert from Bristol CID is drafted in to assist him and Carter with the investigation. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Harry arrives at a harbour brewery to confronts drug dealers who know the whereabouts of a huge stash of heroin. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: TV presenter Adrian Chiles, former Hollyoaks star Gemma Merna (Carmel Valentine), Holby City's Rosie Marcel (Jac Naylor) and broadcaster Jon Snow compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by taking on one of the show's resident trivia experts in a test of their general knowledge. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: Casino Royale (2006)
programme.desc: James Bond achieves his '00' status and is dispatched on the trail of Le Chiffre, an international criminal financing terrorist organisations around the world. Bond's investigation leads to a casino in Montenegro, where he takes part in a high-stakes poker game in a bid to bankrupt Le Chiffre and put an end to his plans. Spy thriller, directed by Martin Campbell and starring Daniel Craig in his first outing as 007, with Eva Green, Mads Mikkelsen, Judi Dench and Giancarlo Giannini

programme.title: Pale Rider (1985)
programme.desc: An unscrupulous landowner threatens to throw gold-miners off their land and take the riches for himself. As the law turns a blind eye to their troubles, the miners desperately need a saviour - who happens to arrive in the form of a mysterious preacher with formidable gunfighting skills. Western, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood, alongside Michael Moriarty, Carrie Snodgress and Richard Dysart

programme.title: A Fistful of Dynamite (1971)
programme.desc: An IRA explosives expert fleeing the British authorities lies low in Mexico, just as the revolution erupts. He is persuaded to join forces with a bandit to rob a bank, but they end up becoming unwitting heroes of the revolutionary cause. Sergio Leone's Spaghetti Western, starring Rod Steiger, James Coburn, Maria Monti, Romolo Valli, Rik Battaglia and Franco Graziosi

programme.title: Duncanville
programme.desc: To celebrate their anniversary, Jack and Annie visit an '80s-themed club, Jing makes Duncan marry her, while Kimberly acts out for attention. Animated comedy revolving around a 15-year-old boy with a rich fantasy life

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Aspiring glamour model Annierose Crone hosts the first dinner party in Coventry, and prepares a menu featuring sausages in the starter, main course and dessert. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Part-time pro-wrestler Joanne Edwards hosts the third party in Coventry. Her risky strategy is to prepare dishes she has never made before, and the lack of knowledge shows when she manages to injure herself twice while cooking the meal

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Publican Tony Jackson hosts the fourth dinner party in Coventry. His guests have high expectations for his pub-themed event and while Michelle Russell thinks he may be the man to beat, Chris Tabor tries to spoil the evening by criticising every aspect of the meal

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Things don't run smoothly as Amy and Sheldon's cohabitation experiment continues, forcing Leonard and Penny to intervene when the couple have an argument about the 'bathroom schedule'. Howard and Bernadette cancel their holiday plans when Bernadette feels unwell and decide to have a secret stay-cation at home instead. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny discovers she has a dedicated group of fans from her Serial Ape-ist days when she accompanies Leonard to a comic-con. Meanwhile, Sheldon and Amy host a brunch at Penny's flat, and stress over the baby's arrival prompts Howard to make an impulsive purchase

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After the repairs are completed in Amy's flat, she lies to Sheldon by telling him that the work is still ongoing so they can carry on living together for a while longer. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa is persuaded to work on her extracurricular activities by her new idol Dahlia Brinkley, Springfield Elementary's only pupil to make it to an Ivy League college. programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa is dumbfounded to discover Marge was an A-grade student, and decides to investigate how she ended up being an ordinary Springfield housewife. Bart accidentally dethrones Nelson as the playground bully, and Maggie becomes obsessed with a collectable elf figurine

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Mr Burns is given six weeks to live, and the town erupts into a party mood. Shocked by their reaction, he decides to take his own life and jumps off a cliff, but fate intervenes and he suffers from a bout of amnesia

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After spending weeks nursing an injured pigeon back to health, Bart is left distraught when Santa's Little Helper eats his feathered friend. Things go from bad to worse for the mischievous greyhound when a psychiatrist recommends the family finds the dog a new home. Featuring the guest voice of Rachel Weisz

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer is persuaded to go undercover to win the trust of Fat Tony as part of an FBI operation to bring the mobster down after an incident involving recycling responsibilities and bribery. With the voice of Jon Hamm (Mad Men)

programme.title: World Chase Tag - USA
programme.desc: Highlights from Atlanta, Georgia, as 16 teams from around America competed for the title in a knockout version of the playground game

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the Cliffemount Hotel in Runswick Bay, North Yorkshire, where general manager Garry Healey and housekeeper Zara Doherty hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to the Quayside Hotel in Boston, Lincolnshire, run by hospitality newbies Alistair Arundel and partner Lana Keptanare

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at Hollington Park Glamping near Newbury, Berkshire, hosted by couple Claire Lennon and Adam De Marco

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Daniele Spiers welcomes her rivals to Swan House in Essex, where a wine tasting threatens to leave a bad taste in the mouth and there is continental confusion at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. The Cliffemount Hotel is first to host and Garry and Zara want to get to grips with Claire and Adam's breakfast grumbles

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Molly makes a life-like farmyard animal when asked that a donkey be the focal point of a surprise 50th birthday cake. Fondant meets fashion for an eighth birthday celebration

programme.title: Heston's Feasts
programme.desc: Reinventing dishes that remind Heston Blumenthal of the 1960s, a decade he believes is best represented by the psychedelia of Roald Dahl's book Charlie and the Chocolate Factory. His themed menu for actress Tamsin Egerton, entertainer Patti Boulaye, singer Mica Paris, TV presenters Tim Lovejoy and Ben Shephard and broadcaster Mike Read features lickable wallpaper, duck a l'orange and a chocolate waterfall with edible rocks

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George's continued renovation mission takes him to Moseley and Wheaton Aston in the West Midlands. He begins with a conversion to unite upstairs and downstairs flats in an Arts and Crafts building, although one of his suggestions leaves the owners speechless. He also visits an 18th-century pub that has fallen victim to some dodgy DIY - and quickly devises a clever plan to make the most of the period features

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.desc: Steve Jones presents action from the battle for pole position in the 14th round of the season from Intercity Istanbul Park. Sebastian Vettel qualified fastest when this venue last hosted a Grand Prix in 2011, but it would have been a surprise if he repeated the feat nine years later given Mercedes' dominance of qualifying this season. With analysis from David Coulthard and Mark Webber

programme.title: Building the World's Fastest Car
programme.desc: Documentary following the team behind a potentially record-breaking vehicle. Having recovered from the project being put into administration with the help of Yorkshire businessman and engineer Ian Warhurst, the team travels to South Africa for six weeks of testing that they hope will attract sponsors to secure the project's future

programme.title: Surviving the Stone Age
programme.desc: The tribe migrates in search of game, having failed to sustain the members through fishing. However, between the experts and what they hope will be a permanent settlement is a mountain range with peaks that stand 2000 metres high, which they must travel over in the midst of an approaching storm using only Stone Age kit and clothing

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores London's Restoration history, including the founding of the Royal Society and the violent dark side behind Charles II's carefully crafted public image. She visits St Paul's Cathedral, reveals how women performed on stage for the first time during this period, and uncovers shocking details of the slave trade at the National Maritime Museum. Plus, a look at parallels between the plague that struck London during the 1660s and the Covid-19 pandemic

programme.title: Red Sparrow (2018)
programme.desc: Ballerina Dominika Egorova is recruited to a Russian intelligence service, where she is forced to use her body as a weapon. Her first mission, targeting a CIA agent, threatens to unravel the security of both nations. Spy thriller, starring Jennifer Lawrence, Joel Edgerton and Matthias Schoenaerts

programme.title: The Ritual (2017)
programme.desc: A group of college friends reunite for a hiking trip in Sweden. When one of them is injured, they are forced to make a risky detour through a forest during a rainstorm, which leads them into the clutches of a menacing presence stalking the woods. Horror based on Adam Nevill's novel, starring Rafe Spall and Robert James-Collier

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef again comes to the aid of failing American restaurants, starting in Fishtown, Philadelphia, where he tries to help the three owners of the Hot Potato Cafe, which has not made a profit in two years. programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Moe achieves massive success when he turns the tavern into a gay bar, but keeps his hetrosexuality a secret to remain in his new clientele's favour. programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Matt Lucas present dessert week of the culinary competition. The signature round features a miniature cheesecake, while the technical challenge features a dish that dates back to the 17th century. Finally, for their showstoppers, the bakers must concoct a jelly art dessert cake constructed of delicate sponge. Judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith are on hand to sample the results and decide who will be Star Baker and who will be leaving the tent

programme.desc: The series investigating residential disputes continues, turning attention to feuds that turned violent. The programme also considers the devastating consequences when personal altercations end up escalating into neighbourhood wars, revealing how a conflict between the Webbs and the Browns in transformed their street in Devon into a war-zone

programme.desc: The comedian ventures off the mainland to the Isle of Bute for a privileged look at what is said to be Scotland's first million-pound home - Mount Stuart House. In Argyll, Susan visits Kilmartin Glen, an ancient valley and home to the Scotti people who gave Scotland its name. She visits a Bronze Age tomb and learns how to make a copper axe in the way they were made thousands of years ago. Then, on the shores of Loch Fyne, she discovers the secrets of oyster cultivation before looking for beavers in the beautiful Knapdale Forest

programme.title: Friday on the Farm
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Jules Hudson present a final look at life on Cannon Hall Farm in South Yorkshire. Farmers Rob and Dave Nicholson call in an expert hoof trimmer to prepare their Highland cows for an indoor winter, go head to head in a traditional horse-drawn ploughing contest, and get a helping hand when it comes to creating novel ways to keep the farm's pigs entertained. Elsewhere, JB Gill visits a Surrey vineyard that produces an English sparkling wine to rival traditional French Champagnes

programme.desc: Special episode exploring how the Owen children have grown over the past five years, and how their unusual childhood on the family's hill farm has shaped them. programme.title: Secrets of the Factories
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes guide to the Mr Kipling Factory in Carlton, South Yorkshire, which first opened in the 1970s and was the largest bakery in Europe at the time. Members of the workforce provide an insight into the cutting edge technology that keeps the cakes coming 24 hours a day, while food historian Polly Russell presents a guide to the company's history, revealing how recipes that date back to the 1800s remain popular favourites to this day

programme.title: Lord Mountbatten: Hero or Villain?
programme.desc: When Louis Mountbatten, the Queen's cousin and Prince Philip's uncle, was murdered by the IRA in 1979 he was lauded as a war hero, an elder statesman and a much-loved member of the royal family. But did the public perception disguise the truth? As a military leader he was responsible for significant loss of life, while as a statesman he was responsible for the badly planned and poorly executed partition of India, which still has repercussions today

programme.title: Jane McDonald & Friends

programme.title: Amazing Cakes & Bakes
programme.desc: The secrets behind one of Britain's most beloved malt loaves, Soreen. Plus, an interview with ex-Apprentice winner Alana, who has been making a name for herself on the Welsh coastline, hand-baking huge quantities of artesian brownies for local cafes and delis. Former art dealer Sebastian makes a birthday cake for Alan Carr, and he has chosen to work from a real-life pig's head, hoping to turn this rather revolting model into a delicious sponge cake for his friend

programme.title: Frankie Howerd: In His Own Words
programme.desc: Frankie Howerd's career highlights are celebrated in this documentary narrated by long-time fan David Walliams. Showcasing Frankie's best bits, it's a journey through the life of the British comedian whose career in showbusiness spanned six decades. It features long forgotten footage, rare performances and personal photographs

programme.title: Paul Merton's Heroes of Comedy
programme.desc: The comedian explores what motivated the world's greatest comics, how and why they became successful, and what inspired their best moments and characters. From Charlie Chaplin, and Laurel and Hardy to Victoria Wood and many more, Paul Merton examines why these comedy greats are so unique

programme.title: Funny Women
programme.desc: Celebrity friends and fans, including James Dreyfus, Wayne Sleep, Christopher Biggins, Michelle Visage and Melissa Manchester, pay homage to the actress and singer. This documentary traces Bette Midler's life from her childhood in Hawaii, her residency at a gay New York bathhouse accompanied on piano by a young Barry Manilow, through an astonishing Hollywood career to a recent record-breaking run on Broadway in Hello Dolly. The life of this pint-sized powerhouse is illustrated with rarely seen archive
